trending_visual_themes: race finish triumph — swimmer at the timing pad, head raised, raw emotion, stadium lights
competition pool atmosphere — blurred crowds, lane ropes, scoreboard glow
teal-and-orange cinematic grade dominating sports photography in 2026
motion blur on water — freeze-frame of splash and streaming water off athlete
low-angle pool deck hero shot — athlete standing on blocks before race, dramatic shadow
recommended_visual_style: Cinematic race-finish — competitive swimmer touching the timing pad, head tilted back, goggles pushed to forehead, water streaming off face, warm orange-amber stadium lighting overhead, deep teal pool water behind, blurred lane ropes and crowd in background, ultra-high contrast, photorealistic, 8K sports photography
content_angle: The wall doesn't lie — the raw emotion of a NZ age group swimmer the instant they touch the timing pad at NAGS 2026
key_insight: Teal-and-orange cinematic color grading is the dominant sports photography trend of 2026, perfectly matching Velocx NZ brand colors. Emotional finish/triumph imagery drives significantly higher engagement than technique or gear content on Facebook, with reaction rates 2–3x higher for high-emotion race-day visuals. NZ Age Group Championships is LIVE right now (April 8–12, Hastings) — making championship celebration content maximally timely on April 10.

nz_event_context: 2026 NZ Age Group Championships is actively running April 8–12 at Hawke's Bay Regional Aquatic Centre in Hastings. Today (April 10) is Day 3 of 5 — the championships are live and NZ swimmers are racing right now. All sessions are being livestreamed on Whakaata Maori YouTube.
